titleStmt.title: Hans Reinhard ("Peppel") Oppel
born Kiel, May 14, 1915; died Darmstadt, March 6(?), 1990

publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: The third natural son of Reinhard Oppel and Gertrud Oppel (née Bandelow): Reinhard’s fifth child.

Hans Reinhard was Reinhard Oppel’s fifth child (the first two children having been adopted), and the third of his second marriage, to Gertrude Bandelow, his older siblings in that marriage being Ulrich and Walter, all three being adversely affected by the death in 1925 of their mother Gertrud.

During Gertrud’s long illness, and after her death, Hans Reinhard was lodged with other member’s of the family, especially Gertrud’s sister in Münster, also with Prof. Klingmüller, a friend of Reinhard. The boy was lazy at school and evidently got into trouble later, as Reinhard remarks in a letter of April 1938 to Jeanette Schenker: “[He] is now indeed fulfilling his military obligation in Darmstadt but is constantly giving me great trouble and financial worries. He was, unfortunately, thoroughly spoiled by his late mother and his aunt, and I must now pay the price for this ...” (OJ 13/10, [198]).
A photograph of him with his two elder siblings is preserved at OJ 72/11, item 4c (May 1921).

listBibl.bibl: Information from Kurt Oppel via Timothy L. Jackson
